sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Frank, presents with a 1-week history of shortness of breath, which has worsened over the past few days.
He reports that this is a recurring issue, as he experienced similar symptoms 6 months ago and was treated with an increased dose of Lasix, which improved his symptoms.
He denies any fever.
Past medical history: Frank has a history of coronary disease, including a heart attack in 2018 for which he underwent stenting of the right coronary artery.
His ejection fraction was reported to be 35%.
He has stage 3 chronic kidney disease and has been taking Lasix 40mg daily.
He reports a history of knee pain and has been advised to avoid Motrin and Advil due to kidney concerns.
Review of systems: Frank reports swelling in his legs, nocturia (1-2 times per night), and occasional nocturnal shortness of breath.
He also reports that his diet has been poor due to holiday gatherings.
Current medications: Lasix 40mg daily, unknown dose and frequency of other medications.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 135/80, unknown pulse rate, oxygen saturation unknown, temperature unknown.
Physical exam: Jugular venous distension 9cm, no carotid bruits, bibasilar crackles with occasional rhonchi, 3/6 systolic ejection murmur at the left base, 1-2+ pitting edema bilaterally.
Test Results: Not mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is experiencing an acute heart failure exacerbation, likely secondary to fluid retention.
Problem list: Heart failure exacerbation; Coronary artery disease; Stage 3 chronic kidney disease; Possible gout flare.
Plan:
I prescribed Lasix 80mg daily, with instructions to take it in the morning and reassess in 2 days.
I ordered a chest X-ray to rule out any other cardiac issues.
I also ordered an echocardiogram to assess the heart murmur and ejection fraction.
I referred Frank to a dietitian to help manage his diet and potassium levels.
I instructed him to avoid Motrin and Advil and to use Tylenol for pain management instead.
I ordered a complete metabolic panel to assess his kidney function.
Follow-up: I will re-evaluate Frank in 2 days to assess his response to the increased Lasix dose and to reassess his fluid status.
